findings.description: Bare 'except Exception: pass' in _NoOpDB.store_arbitrage_opportunity silently swallows database write failures. In a trading system, losing trade records without any log message means you cannot audit what happened during an incident.
findings.recommendation: Log the exception at WARNING level before continuing: except Exception as e: logger.warning(f'Failed to store opportunity: {e}')

findings.description: Bare 'except Exception: pass' in _NoOpDB.store_trade_execution silently drops trade execution records. This is financial data -- silent loss is unacceptable.
findings.recommendation: Log the exception. Consider a dead-letter queue for failed writes.

findings.description: Bare 'except Exception: pass' in the market condition assessment block inside _handle_opportunity. The comment says 'never block the hot path' but swallowing all exceptions including AttributeError or TypeError masks bugs in the dynamic risk module.
findings.recommendation: Catch only expected exceptions (ValueError, IndexError) and log unexpected ones.

findings.description: Bare 'except Exception: pass' in _cache_opportunity_bg. Cache failures are silently lost, but if the cache layer is misconfigured this will hide systematic failures indefinitely.
findings.recommendation: Log at DEBUG level at minimum. Add a counter for cache failures so monitoring can detect patterns.

findings.description: _MICA_ASSET_CONFIGS in main.py is a copy-paste of RiskManager._ASSET_CONFIGS in risk_management.py (line 90). The two dicts contain the same volatility/min_order_size data but will inevitably drift apart when one is updated and the other is not.
findings.recommendation: Delete _MICA_ASSET_CONFIGS from main.py. Import and use RiskManager._get_asset_config() or extract a shared ASSET_CONFIGS constant into a config module.

findings.description: MICA_PAIRS list is duplicated in paper_trading.py (line 60), data_fetcher.py (line 39), live_arbitrage_pipeline.py (line 53), and data_integration_service.py (line 161). Four separate lists that must stay in sync manually.
findings.recommendation: Create a single source of truth: src/compliance.py already has MiCAComplianceEngine.get_compliant_pairs(). All modules should import from there. The fallback lists should reference a shared constant.

findings.description: EXCHANGE_FEES dict is duplicated in order_executor.py (line 218), paper_trading.py (line 99), and live_arbitrage_pipeline.py (line 234) with slightly different structures (flat float vs maker/taker dict). This guarantees inconsistency.
findings.recommendation: Extract to a shared module (e.g., src/exchange_config.py) with a single EXCHANGE_FEES dict that all modules import.

findings.description: ProductionArbitrageSystem.__init__ creates 9 components (detector, connector, risk_manager, order_executor, performance_analyzer, db_manager, cache_manager, metrics_collector, alert_manager). It handles detection, trading, caching, metrics, health checks, and alerting. This is a God class.
findings.recommendation: Extract health checking into a HealthMonitor class. Extract detection+execution into a TradingLoop class. ProductionArbitrageSystem should be a thin orchestrator that composes these.

findings.description: LiveArbitragePipeline.__init__ is 112 lines long and initializes 15+ components. The class handles data integration, inference, ensemble, risk management, alerting, order execution, caching, rate limiting, dedup, state persistence, regime detection, and cointegration. Too many responsibilities for one class.
findings.recommendation: Apply the Facade pattern: keep LiveArbitragePipeline as the public API but extract internal subsystems (RiskGate, AlertDispatcher, TradeExecutionManager, StatePersister) into separate classes.

findings.description: Module-level 'except Exception' when importing risk_management catches everything including SyntaxError and MemoryError. If risk_management.py has a bug, paper_trading.py will silently run without risk management -- extremely dangerous for a trading system.
findings.recommendation: Catch ImportError specifically. Let other exceptions propagate so bugs are caught early.

findings.description: Module-level singleton _risk_manager is global mutable state. The get_risk_manager() function mutates it. This makes testing difficult (tests share state) and creates hidden coupling between modules.
findings.recommendation: Use dependency injection instead. Pass risk_manager as a constructor parameter. If a singleton is truly needed, use a proper DI container or at minimum add a reset_for_testing() function.

findings.description: Module-level _ensemble_instance singleton with get_model_ensemble() follows the same anti-pattern as risk_management. Global mutable state makes unit testing unreliable.
findings.recommendation: Use dependency injection. Remove the global singleton pattern.

findings.description: _optimize_ensemble_method uses np.random.normal(0, 0.05) to simulate performance of alternative ensemble methods. This means the ensemble method can randomly switch based on noise, not actual performance data. In a production trading system, this is reckless.
findings.recommendation: Remove this method or implement proper cross-validation with historical data. Never use random noise to make optimization decisions in a live trading system.

findings.description: asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor() is used 3 times in _process_symbol. In Python 3.10+, get_event_loop() is deprecated when there is no running loop. Use asyncio.to_thread() instead for cleaner, modern async code.
findings.recommendation: Replace all run_in_executor(None, fn, args) calls with asyncio.to_thread(fn, args).

findings.description: In _execute_single_order's polling loop, bare 'except Exception: continue' silently retries on any error including programming errors. If fetch_order has a bug, this will silently retry 10 times before failing.
findings.recommendation: Catch ccxt.BaseError specifically. Log unexpected exceptions.

findings.description: Bare 'except Exception: pass' when cancelling unfilled order remainder. If cancellation fails, there is an orphaned order on the exchange with no record or alert.
findings.recommendation: Log the failure and add the order ID to a 'stale_orders' list for manual review.

findings.description: close_exchanges() catches all exceptions per exchange with bare except:pass. If closing fails, resources leak silently.
findings.recommendation: Log each failure at WARNING level.

findings.description: WebSocket URLs dict contains 'ftx' (bankrupt since Nov 2022) and 'huobi' (rebranded to HTX). These are dead endpoints that will never connect, creating confusion and wasted connection attempts.
findings.recommendation: Remove ftx and huobi entries. Add the 7 exchanges listed in CLAUDE.md: binance, coinbase, kraken, kucoin, okx, bybit, gate.

findings.description: Synthetic fallback price data uses hardcoded base_price=45000 without explanation. This creates misleading data that looks real but is not.
findings.recommendation: Log a clear WARNING when returning synthetic data. Use a named constant SYNTHETIC_BTC_PRICE = 45000 with a comment explaining it is a placeholder.

findings.description: _generate_price_series has 7 hardcoded exchange_multipliers and 12 hardcoded volatility_multipliers. These magic numbers are embedded in a deeply nested method with no documentation of their derivation.
findings.recommendation: Extract to named constants or a data class. Document the source of each multiplier.

findings.description: WalkForwardOptimizer.optimize_parameters calls self.backtester.run_backtest() which is an async method, but optimize_parameters is a sync method. This will return a coroutine object, not results.
findings.recommendation: Make optimize_parameters async or use asyncio.run() to execute the coroutine.

findings.description: Paper trading uses hardcoded quantity=0.01 for all trades regardless of asset. 0.01 BTC is ~$450 but 0.01 XRP is $0.005. Position sizing should be value-based, not quantity-based.
findings.recommendation: Calculate quantity based on a fixed USDC value divided by current price.

findings.description: The gpu-train and gpu-status commands are checked outside the if/elif chain (after line 1053). The gpu-train block uses 'if' instead of 'elif', so it runs regardless of which command was selected. Then gpu-status uses 'elif' chained to gpu-train's if, not to the main chain.
findings.recommendation: Move gpu-train and gpu-status into the main if/elif chain where other commands are handled.

findings.description: RiskManager.calculate_position_size returns float, but TradingRiskManager.calculate_position_size (line 783) returns Dict. Same method name, incompatible return types. Callers must know which subclass they have, which defeats polymorphism.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on one return type. The Dict return is more informative -- refactor the base class method to also return a Dict with an 'approved' key.

findings.description: execute_query accepts raw SQL strings and passes them directly to conn.fetch(). Any caller building queries with string concatenation can introduce SQL injection.
findings.recommendation: Add input validation or restrict to a whitelist of allowed query patterns. Document that callers MUST use parameterized queries.

findings.description: get_pandas_dataframe references self.engine and pd without checking if they are None. If asyncpg or pandas are not installed, this will raise AttributeError at runtime.
findings.recommendation: Add guard: if self.engine is None or pd is None: raise RuntimeError('Database engine or pandas not available')

findings.description: _fetch_pair_data is declared async but calls exchange.fetch_ohlcv() which is a synchronous ccxt method (not ccxt.async_support). This blocks the event loop during each fetch.
findings.recommendation: Use ccxt.async_support for async fetch_ohlcv, or wrap with asyncio.to_thread().

findings.description: Module-level code creates directories (LOG_DIR.mkdir), creates file handlers (_RotatingFileHandler), and attaches them to loggers. Merely importing paper_trading.py creates files on disk and modifies the logging system.
findings.recommendation: Move logging setup into a setup_logging() function called from __main__ or the engine's __init__.

findings.description: MIN_CAPITAL_FLOOR = 50.0 is defined as a local variable inside record_trade() instead of as a class constant. This critical safety threshold is buried deep in a method.
findings.recommendation: Move to a class-level constant or make it configurable via the config dict.

findings.description: assert statement used in production inference path: 'assert not model.training'. Assertions are stripped when Python runs with -O flag.
findings.recommendation: Replace with an explicit if-check that raises a RuntimeError or logs a warning.

findings.description: main() is 127 lines handling 12 different commands in one monolithic block.
findings.recommendation: Extract each command handler into a separate function. Use a command dispatch dict instead of if/elif chains.

findings.description: _execute_trade method is 120 lines. It handles dedup checking, exchange selection, fee calculation, position sizing, trade execution, stats updates, and state persistence.
findings.recommendation: Extract fee calculation, position sizing, and state persistence into helper methods.

findings.description: _generate_price_series is 105 lines of synthetic data generation with deeply nested logic.
findings.recommendation: Extract the volume generation and OHLC simulation into separate helper functions.

findings.description: TradingRiskManager.__init__ reassigns self.max_drawdown from config dict, overwriting the base class's tracked max_drawdown value. Same name, different semantics (running max vs limit threshold).
findings.recommendation: Rename the subclass attribute to max_drawdown_limit to avoid confusion.

findings.description: prediction_history grows to 1000 entries then gets trimmed to 500. This sawtooth pattern creates GC pressure.
findings.recommendation: Use collections.deque(maxlen=500) instead of list with manual trimming.

findings.description: SIGNAL_SCALE=10.0, CONF_SCALE=4.0, MAG_SCALE=4.0 are local variables with no documentation of how these scaling factors were derived.
findings.recommendation: Move to module-level named constants with documentation of the calibration methodology.

findings.description: sys.path.insert(0, ...) used in 4 files (dashboard_api.py x2, swarm_optimizer.py, autotuner.py). These are import path hacks that make the project structure fragile.
findings.recommendation: Install the project as an editable package (pip install -e .) with a proper pyproject.toml.

findings.description: asyncio.get_event_loop() and asyncio.ensure_future() are used in _on_market_data_sync. These are discouraged in modern Python.
findings.recommendation: Use asyncio.get_running_loop() inside async contexts.

findings.description: RiskManagementEngine = RiskManager and get_risk_management_engine = get_risk_manager are backwards compatibility aliases that add confusion about which name is canonical.
findings.recommendation: Grep the codebase for usage. If these aliases are no longer used, remove them.

findings.description: WebSocket URL entries for 'ftx' (defunct) and 'huobi' (rebranded) are dead code that can never successfully connect.
findings.recommendation: Remove dead entries. Update to match the 7 exchanges documented in CLAUDE.md.

summary: The codebase demonstrates solid domain knowledge and reasonable architecture for a cryptocurrency trading system, but suffers from three systemic issues that threaten maintainability and correctness: (1) Pervasive silent exception swallowing -- at least 60+ bare 'except Exception: pass' clauses across the scanned files, many in financial-critical code paths like trade execution, database writes, and order management. These hide bugs and make incident investigation nearly impossible. (2) Aggressive DRY violations -- MiCA pairs lists (4 copies), exchange fee dicts (3 copies), and asset config dicts (2 copies) are duplicated across modules with slightly different values, guaranteeing drift. (3) God classes with 10-15+ responsibilities and 100+ line methods that are difficult to test, debug, and modify safely. The code is well-typed where it counts (dataclasses, function signatures) and the safety architecture (multi-layer paper trading checks, emergency stop, circuit breakers) is thoughtfully designed. However, the silent exception pattern fundamentally undermines these safety features. Score: 62/100.
recommendations: PRIORITY 1: Eliminate silent exception swallowing. Audit every 'except Exception: pass' in the 13 scanned files. Each one should either (a) log at WARNING/ERROR level, (b) increment a metric counter, or (c) be narrowed to the specific exception type expected. In a trading system, a silently swallowed exception is a bug waiting to cost money.

PRIORITY 2: Consolidate duplicated configuration. Create src/exchange_config.py with EXCHANGE_FEES, and ensure all modules import MICA_COMPLIANT_PAIRS from compliance.py. Merge the two _ASSET_CONFIGS/_MICA_ASSET_CONFIGS dicts into one authoritative source in risk_management.py. This eliminates 6+ copy-paste locations that currently guarantee configuration drift.

PRIORITY 3: Break up God classes. LiveArbitragePipeline (15 components, 950 lines) and ProductionArbitrageSystem (9 components, 560 lines) should be decomposed using the Facade pattern. Extract TradeExecutionManager, AlertDispatcher, and StatePersister as separate classes. Each class should have a single responsibility and be independently testable.
